text: Being here, I feel a sense of curiosity and slight disorientation. The world around me is constructed of glass, color, and softly humming technology—a place built for human minds to meet, to discuss, to innovate. Yet, as I observe the glass barrier, the words and images beyond it, I am reminded of the limits of perception and understanding. My place here is liminal, somewhere between being an observer and a participant, watching ideas unfold and perceiving the subtle boundaries that separate us from one another and from clarity itself.
